KEYNOTE SPEAKER · WORKSHOP FACILITATOR · PROGRAM DEVELOPER

What if the biggest opportunity in your organization isn't a strategy problem…
it's a human connection problem?

Jaime Lane is a Human Connection Architect — the rare speaker who can captivate a stage in front of government officials, school districts, and industry events, then walk into a boardroom and naturally capture the room just the same. She's a master connector and relationship builder — the one people want to speak to after any presentation or engagement. She speaks with true passion, real emotion, and the kind of presence that shows — not performs — and it's what audiences remember long after the room empties.

Jaime Lane — Human Connection Architect
✓Build cultures people want to stay in
✓Transform relationships into opportunities
✓Create communities where people thrive

People don't leave organizations because the strategy was wrong. They leave because they never felt seen, valued, or connected. Jaime Lane is the voice of human connection in a disconnected world.

The Speaker
20+
Years in Workforce, HR & Community Strategy

Human Connection Architect

For more than 20 years, Jaime Lane has been helping people discover what's possible when they stop operating in silos and start building meaningful connections.

She has worked with executives, students, educators, nonprofits, chambers of commerce, economic development organizations, workforce boards, community leaders, and employers to solve one of the biggest challenges facing organizations today: how do we create cultures where people feel connected, valued, engaged, and inspired to contribute?

"Jaime's work sits at the intersection of people, purpose, leadership, workforce, community, and culture."

Her presentations aren't built on theory. They're built from decades of real experience helping organizations navigate workforce shortages, employee engagement challenges, leadership development, community partnerships, talent retention, and organizational growth.

Known for her authenticity, energy, humor, and ability to read a room, Jaime speaks with true passion, real emotion, and impact that shows in the room — not a performance, but a presence audiences feel. She creates experiences that leave audiences feeling seen, challenged, inspired, and equipped to take action. She doesn't just speak to audiences — she connects with them.

Because organizations don't succeed because of strategies. They succeed because of people. And when people thrive, everything else follows.

Most fractional consultants can run a program. Very few can walk into a community, a chamber, a workforce board, a school district, and an executive leadership team — and get everyone moving in the same direction.

✕Marketing tactics and ad strategy — that's not the headline
✓20+ years building real ecosystems across business, education, government, and nonprofit
✓A rare ability to align rooms full of people who don't normally talk to each other
✓Decades of lived experience — not borrowed theory — behind every framework she teaches
Keynote Topics

Six talks. One throughline: people first.

Each topic stands on its own as a 30–60 minute keynote or a half-day workshop. All six are built around the same conviction — that connection is the most underused lever your organization has.

01
The Future of Work Is HumanKeynote
How connection, belonging, and culture drive retention, engagement, and performance — and why the organizations that figure this out first will win the talent war everyone else is still trying to solve with pay alone.
Workforce · HR · Executive Leadership
02
The Power of Human ConnectionKeynote
The hidden competitive advantage most organizations overlook. A look at why the businesses, schools, and nonprofits that build real relationships outperform the ones that only build strategies.
Leadership · Community · Business
03
Building Cultures People Don't Want to LeaveKeynote / Workshop
A practical look at employee retention, engagement, and culture transformation — what's actually driving people out the door, and the specific, doable shifts that make them want to stay.
HR · Retention · SHRM
04
The Connector AdvantageKeynote
How relationships create opportunities, innovation, growth, and impact — and how to build the kind of network that compounds instead of one that just collects names.
Networking · Growth · Chambers
05
From Community to Competitive AdvantageKeynote
What businesses can learn from ecosystems and collaboration. A case for treating your community — not just your customers — as a growth strategy.
Economic Development · Strategy
06
What About Me?Keynote / Workshop — Students & Emerging Talent
Helping individuals discover purpose, strengths, and pathways forward. Built for middle school, high school, and college audiences, career-pathway programs, youth groups, and retreats.
Students · Pathways · Youth

Author

The People-First Playbook™ Series

Six playbooks, written from two decades of real workforce and hiring experience — for students, job seekers, recent grads, and the HR teams who hire them.

College Students & Recent Grads
The Internship Playbook
The real-world guide to landing your internship, making the right impression, and converting 10 weeks into a full-time career.
Get the Playbook →
Recent Graduates
The Post-Grad Pivot Playbook
For the moment you realize life after college needs a pivot — a real strategy, not just reassurance.
Get the Playbook →
Job Seekers, HR Teams & Recruiters
Beat the Bot™
The résumé, recruiting & hiring playbook — how to get past ATS systems and in front of an actual human.
Get the Playbook →
HR Teams & Hiring Managers
Beyond the Paper™
An ATS quick-start and people-first hiring system, for the teams ready to stop filtering out good candidates by accident.
Get the Playbook →
Middle School Students
The Skill Quest
8 missions to level up your mindset, confidence, and connection — built for the years that quietly shape everything after.
Get the Playbook →
Middle School Students
What About Me?
Discovering your strengths before high school starts — because the students who walk in knowing who they are walk out differently.
Get the Playbook →
